content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
September 21,
1993
Chip Nielsen
Nielsen,
Merksamer, Parrinello,
Mueller
& Naylor
591 Redwood Highway,
No.
4000
Mill Valley,
CA 94941
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-93-321
Dear Mr.
Nielsen:
You have requested advice on behalf of the Pete Wilson for
Governor Committee
(the “Committee”)
under the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”) •1/
OUESTION
Must the Committee submit copies of its campaign statements
to the Registrar of Voters for Sacramento or San Diego Counties?
ANSWER
The Governor and his controlled committee must file copies of
their consolidated campaign statements
(Form 490),
with the
Registrar of Voters for both Sacramento and San Diego Counties.
FACTS
On July 1,
1993,
you became treasurer for the Pete Wilson for
Governor Committee.
The original and one copy of its semi—annual
Form 490 for the first half of
1993 were filed with the Secretary
of State and two copies were filed with San Francisco,
Los Angeles
and Sacramento Counties.
The Committee’s previous treasurer was
informed by the Office of the Registrar of Voters for San Diego
County that the Committee should file copies of its statements
with the San Diego County Registrar of Voters.
It is our
1/
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
et q.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street. Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807
•
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807
•
(916) 322-5660


Nielsen,
Chip
A—93—32 1
Page2
understanding that the San Diego County Registrar based this
request on the fact that Governor Wilson’s domicile for voting
purposes,
as defined by Elections Code Section 200,
has been and
remains in San Diego County.
The Governor and the Committee file
consolidated campaign statements.
The Committee’s address as
shown on its statement of organization is in Sacramento County and
therefore it is domiciled in Sacramento County.
The Governor
resides and works in Sacramento County.
ANALYSIS
Government Code Section 84215 provides:
All candidates, elected officers,
committees and
proponents of state ballot measures or the qualifications of
state ballot measures,
except as provided in subdivision
(e)
shall file two copies of the campaign statements required by
Section 84200 with the clerk of the county in which they are
domiciled.
A committee is domiciled at the address listed on
its campaign statement unless it is domiciled outside
California in which case its domicile shall be deemed to be
Los Angeles for the purpose of this section.
In addition,
campaign statements shall be filed at the following places:
(a)
Statewide elected officers,
candidates for these
offices,
supreme court justices, their controlled
committees,
committees formed or existing primarily to
support or oppose these candidates, elected officers,
supreme court justices,
or statewide measures,
or the
qualifications of state ballot measures,
and all state
general purpose committees and filers not specified in
subdivisions
(b)
to
(e),
inclusive:
(1)
The original and one copy with the Secretary
of State.
(2)
TWO copies with the Registrar—Recorder of Los
Angeles County.
(3)
Two copies with the Registrar—Recorder of the
City and County of
San Francisco...
(Emphasis added.)
Government Code Section 84209 provides:
A candidate or state measure proponent and any committee
or committees which the candidate or a state measure
proponent controls may file consolidated campaign statements
under this chapter.
Such consolidated statements shall be
filed in each place each of the committees and the candidate
or state measure proponent would be required to file campaign
statements if separate statements were filed.


Nielsen,
Chip
A—93—321
Page
3
Although Section 84215 defines “domicile” for committees, it
does not do so for candidates.
Black’s Law Dictionary defines
“domicile” as:
[T)hat place where a man has his true,
fixed,
and
permanent home and principal establishment,
and to which
whenever he is absent he has the intention of returning.
Smith v.
Smith,
206 Pa.
Super.
310,
213 A.2d 94.
The
permanent residence of a person or the place to which he
intends to return even though he may actually reside
elsewhere.
A person may have more than one residence but
only one domicile....
California case law is consistent with this definition.
(See, e.g., Whittell v.
Franchise Tax Board
(1965)
231 Cal.App.2d
278.)
You have not indicated whether Governor Wilson considers San
Diego to be his permanent residence.
However,
based on the above
definition and the fact that Governor Wilson is domiciled for
voting purposes in San Diego County
(see Elections Code Section
200),
we are advising that San Diego County is his county of
domicile for determining where copies of his consolidated campaign
statements should be filed.
Because the Committee’s address is in
Sacramento,
copies of the consolidated campaign statments must
also be filed in Sacramento County.
If you have additional questions,
please contact me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Steve Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Wayne .
Imberi
c
2
-u.)
Po1itcal Reform Consultant
